chief_complaint: A 64-year-old male patient presented with neck stiffness, headache, fever.
history: The patient had eosinophilic meningitis caused by Angiostrongylus cantonensis, an uncommon disease in Brazil. He ingested slugs, water or food contaminated with the parasite. He had a false-positive result for Neisseria meningitides and received unsuccessful treatment with antibiotics. He was treated with prednisone and albendazole after extensive investigation.
exam: Neck stiffness, headache, fever, and peripheral and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) eosinophilia were observed.
labs: DNA and anti-A.cantonensis antibodies were detected in CSF. Detection of antibodies and DNA of the parasite in CSF was crucial for diagnosis.
correct_diagnosis: Eosinophilic meningitis caused by Angiostrongylus cantonensis
differentials: Eosinophilic meningitis caused by Angiostrongylus cantonensis
correct_ranking: Eosinophilic meningitis caused by Angiostrongylus cantonensis
